# AEGetDescDataSize(_:)

**Function**

Gets the size, in bytes, of the data in the specified descriptor.

## Declaration

```swift
func AEGetDescDataSize(_ theAEDesc: UnsafePointer<AEDesc>!) -> Size
```

## Parameters

- **theAEDesc**: A pointer to the descriptor to obtain the data size for. See [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/aedesc].

## Return Value

Returns the size, in bytes, of the data in the specified descriptor.

## Discussion

This function works only with value descriptors created by [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1448535-aecreatedesc]. You cannot get the data size of an [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/aerecord] or [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/aedesclist], for example.

### Version-Notes

Thread safe starting in OS X v10.2.

## Operating On Descriptor Data

- **AEGetDescData(_:_:_:)**: Gets the data from the specified descriptor.
- **AEGetDescDataRange(_:_:_:_:)**: Retrieves a specified series of bytes from the specified descriptor.
- **AEReplaceDescData(_:_:_:_:)**: Copies the specified data into the specified descriptor, replacing any previous data.
